devanagari: केशिवधः नारदकृतं भगवतस्तवनं निलायन क्रीडायां व्योमासुरवधश्च । श्रीशुक उवाच । केशी तु कंसप्रहितः खुरैर्महीं । महाहयो निर्जरयन्मनोजवः । स...
english_devnagari: keśivadhaḥ nāradakṛtaṃ bhagavatastavanaṃ nilāyana krīḍāyāṃ vyomāsuravadhaśca śrīśuka uvāca keśī tu kaṃsaprahitaḥ khurairmahīṃ mahāhayo nirjarayanmanojavaḥ saṭāvadhūtābhravimānasaṅkulaṃ kurvannabho heṣitabhīṣitākhilaḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Demon Keśin who was deputed by Kaṃsa, assumed the form of a gigantic horse with the speed of the mind. Furrowing the earth with his hoofs, overcrowding the sky with clouds and heavenly cars dispersed by the waving of his mane and terrifying all creatures by his neighing he came to Nanda’s Vraja.
1(A).[1] With big eyes, a terribly vast cavern-like mouth, an enormous neck resembling a large dark-blue cloud, the wicked-hearted Keśin, desirous of achieving the desired object of Kaṃsa, rushed to the Vraja of Nanda, making it tremble with fear.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Not found in the text of Bhāvāratha Dīpikā but recorded in the texts of Bhāgavata Candrikā, Padaratnāvalī, Subodhinī and Siddhāntapradīpa Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava annotators follow Bhāvāratha Dīpikā and do not comment on it.

devanagari: विशालनेत्रो विकटास्यकोटरो । बृहद्गलो नीलमहाम्बुदोपमः । दुराशयः कंसहितं चिकीर्षुः । व्रजं स नम्दस्य जगाम कम्पयन् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: viśālanetro vikaṭāsyakoṭaro bṛhadgalo nīlamahāmbudopamaḥ durāśayaḥ kaṃsahitaṃ cikīrṣuḥ vrajaṃ sa namdasya jagāma kampayan

translation: Occupying a forward position, the glorious Lord challenged Keśin who was creating a panic among the inhabitants of his Gokula by his neighing and was disturbing the formation of clouds in the sky by his tail and was seeking a fight with him—at which Keśin roared like a lion.

devanagari: तं त्रासयन्तं भगवान् स्वगोकुलं । तद्धेषितैर्वालविघूर्णिताम्बुदम् । आत्मानमाजौ मृगयन्तमग्रणीः । उपाह्वयत् स व्यनदन् मृगेन्द्रवत् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ trāsayantaṃ bhagavān svagokulaṃ taddheṣitairvālavighūrṇitāmbudam ātmānamājau mṛgayantamagraṇīḥ upāhvayat sa vyanadan mṛgendravat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [imperfect active third plural]

translation: Beholding Kṛṣṇa confronting him, that unapproachable formidable beast of terrific speed rushed at him with extreme rage, drinking up the heavens, as it were, with his wide open mouth (and turning round) kicked hard with his hind legs the Lord with lotus-eyes.

devanagari: स तं निशाम्याभिमुखो मखेन खं । पिबन्निवाभ्यद्रवदत्यमर्षणः । जघान पद्‍भ्यामरविन्दलोचनं । दुरासदश्चण्डजवो दुरत्ययः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa taṃ niśāmyābhimukho makhena khaṃ pibannivābhyadravadatyamarṣaṇaḥ jaghāna pad‍bhyāmaravindalocanaṃ durāsadaścaṇḍajavo duratyayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pā class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Dodging the Kick, Kṛṣṇa, the Lord (who is suprasense perception) angrily caught hold of his two hind legs with his hands and whirling him round threw him contemptuously to a distance of hundred bows (cubits), even as Garuḍa would fling (an ordinary) serpent, and stood as before.

devanagari: तद् वञ्चयित्वा तमधोक्षजो रुषा । प्रगृह्य दोर्भ्यां परिविध्य पादयोः । सावज्ञमुत्सृज्य धनुःशतान्तरे । यथोरगं तार्क्ष्यसुतो व्यवस्थितः ॥ ५...
english_devnagari: tad vañcayitvā tamadhokṣajo ruṣā pragṛhya dorbhyāṃ parividhya pādayoḥ sāvajñamutsṛjya dhanuḥśatāntare yathoragaṃ tārkṣyasuto vyavasthitaḥ
translation: Regaining his consciousness, Keśin sprang to his feet. Opening his mouth wide in violent wrath, he fiercely attacked Hari. Smiling fearlessly, Hari thrust his left arm into Keśin’s mouth as a serpent enters a hole.

devanagari: सः लब्धसंज्ञः पुनरुत्थितो रुषा । व्यादाय केशी तरसाऽपतद्धरिम् । सोऽप्यस्य वक्त्रे भुजमुत्तरं स्मयन् । प्रवेशयामास यथोरगं बिले ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: saḥ labdhasaṃjñaḥ punarutthito ruṣā vyādāya keśī tarasā so praveśayāmāsa yathoragaṃ bile

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: When Keśin tried to bite Kṛṣṇa’s arms his teeth dropped down at the touch of the Lord’s arm as if at the touch of red-hot iron. And the Lord’s arm thrust in the interior part of his body went on growing immensely like dropsy, when neglected.

devanagari: दन्ता निपेतुर्भगवद्‍भुजस्पृशः । ते केशिनस्तप्तमयस्पृशो यथा । बाहुश्च तद्देहगतो महात्मनो । यथाऽमयः संववृधे उपेक्षितः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: dantā nipeturbhagavad‍bhujaspṛśaḥ te keśinastaptamayaspṛśo yathā bāhuśca taddehagato mahātmano yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ tap class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ tap class 4 verb]

[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: With his breath (passage) choked by the (disproportionately) swelling arm of Kṛṣṇa, he kicked up his legs, perspired profusely all over the body, and with its eyes rolling, discharging excretion, he fell down dead on the ground.

devanagari: समेधमानेन स कृष्णबाहुना । निरुद्धवायुश्चरणांश्च विक्षिपन् । प्रस्विन्नगात्रः परिवृत्तलोचनः । पपात लेण्डं विसृजन् क्षितौ व्यसुः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: samedhamānena sa kṛṣṇabāhunā niruddhavāyuścaraṇāṃśca vikṣipan prasvinnagātraḥ parivṛttalocanaḥ papāta leṇḍaṃ visṛjan kṣitau vyasuḥ

[nominative dual from √ kṣī class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṣī class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṣī class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ kṣī class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṣī class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṣī class 5 verb], [nominative dual from √ kṣī class 9 verb], [vocative dual from √ kṣī class 9 verb], [accusative dual from √ kṣī class 9 verb]

translation: Extricating his arm from out of the body of Keśī (which was burst open at various places) like a ripe cucumber, the mightly-armed Kṛṣṇa was not even slightly touched with pride, even though he effortlessly killed his enemies, but was glorified in songs and showered over with flowers by the wondering gods.

devanagari: तद्देहतः कर्कटिकाफलोपमाद् । व्यसोरपाकृष्य भुजं महाभुजः । अविस्मितोऽयत्‍नहतारिरुत्स्मयैः । प्रसूनवर्षैर्दिविषद्भिरीडितः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: taddehataḥ karkaṭikāphalopamād vyasorapākṛṣya bhujaṃ mahābhujaḥ avismito prasūnavarṣairdiviṣadbhirīḍitaḥ
translation: Oh king! Thereafter the divine sage Nārada, the foremost of devotees of the Lord, approached Kṛṣṇa of noble behaviour and conferred with him in privacy as follows:

devanagari: देवर्षिरुपसङ्गम्य भागवतप्रवरो नृप । कृष्णमक्लिष्टकर्माणं रहस्येतदभाषत ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: devarṣirupasaṅgamya bhāgavatapravaro nṛpa kṛṣṇamakliṣṭakarmāṇaṃ rahasyetadabhāṣata
translation: “Oh Kṛṣṇa (the enchanter of the world)! Oh Kṛṣṇa of incomprehensible nature! Oh Lord of yogi! The absolute Ruler of the world! Oh Vāsudeva (the pervader of the universe) and the abode of all! Oh the most prominent among the Sātvatas (the Yāḍava clan)! Oh Almighty Lord![2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: Nārada glosses over the disclosure about Kṛṣṇa to Kaṃsa by telling Him, “You are the Almighty Lord specially incarnated for relieving the earth of its burden of Daityas. Now You be active, finish with them and protect the Universe.”

devanagari: कृष्ण कृष्णाप्रमेयात्मन् योगेश जगदीश्वर । वासुदेवाखिलावास सात्वतां प्रवर प्रभो ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇāprameyātman yogeśa jagadīśvara vāsudevākhilāvāsa sātvatāṃ pravara prabho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Like unto fire lying dormant in fuel, you alone are the Inner Controller of all beings. Remaining unrevealed, you abide in the hearts of every being as a witness to everything. You are the Supreme Person, the Controller of the universe.

devanagari: त्वमात्मा सर्वभूतानां एको ज्योतिरिवैधसाम् । गूढो गुहाशयः साक्षी महापुरुष ईश्वरः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tvamātmā sarvabhūtānāṃ eko jyotirivaidhasām gūḍho guhāśayaḥ sākṣī mahāpuruṣa īśvaraḥ
translation: Depending entirely upon your own Self, You evolved guṇas through your Māyā potency. The Almighty Lord of unfailing will power as you are, you create, protect, and devour (destroy) this universe with those guṇas.

devanagari: आत्मनाऽत्माश्रयः पूर्वं मायया ससृजे गुणान् । तैरिदं सत्यसङ्कल्पः सृजस्यत्स्यवसीश्वरः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmanā tairidaṃ satyasaṅkalpaḥ sṛjasyatsyavasīśvaraḥ
translation: Such as you are, you have incarnated yourself on the earth for the annihilation of the daityas (demons), goblins and rākṣasas that have been born as kings and for protecting those who abide by the canons of righteousness (or your votaries.)

devanagari: स त्वं भूधरभूतानां दैत्यप्रमथरक्षसाम् । अवतीर्णो विनाशाय साधुनां रक्षणाय च ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tvaṃ bhūdharabhūtānāṃ daityapramatharakṣasām avatīrṇo vināśāya sādhunāṃ rakṣaṇāya ca
translation: Fortunately enough this demon Keśin who assumed the form of a horse and terrifically frightened at whose neighing, gods (the eye-lid-less beings) deserted the heaven, has been easily killed by you, as in a sport.

devanagari: दिष्ट्या ते निहतो दैत्यो लीलयायं हयाकृतिः । यस्य हेषितसन्त्रस्ताः त्यजन्त्यनिमिषा दिवम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: diṣṭyā te nihato daityo līlayāyaṃ hayākṛtiḥ yasya heṣitasantrastāḥ tyajantyanimiṣā divam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb]

translation: Oh Omnipresent Lord I Day after tomorrow, I shall witness Cāṇūra, Muṣṭika and other wrestlers, as also the elephant (Kuvalayāpīḍa) as well as Kaṃsa slain by you in broad day-light.

devanagari: चाणूरं मुष्टिकं चैव मल्लानन्यांश्च हस्तिनम् । कंसं च निहतं द्रक्ष्ये परश्वोऽहनि ते विभो ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: cāṇūraṃ muṣṭikaṃ caiva mallānanyāṃśca hastinam kaṃsaṃ ca nihataṃ drakṣye paraśvo
translation: Thereafter I shall see the slaying of the demon Pañcajana (who was a conch-dweller) Kālayavana, Mura and Naraka as well as bringing down (from heaven the celestial tree) Pārijāta after defeating Indra.

devanagari: तस्यानु शङ्खयवन मुराणां नरकस्य च । पारिजातापहरणं इन्द्रस्य च पराजयम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyānu śaṅkhayavana murāṇāṃ narakasya ca pārijātāpaharaṇaṃ indrasya ca parājayam
translation: After that I shall witness your marriages with the daughters of warriors (like Bhīṣmaka, Rukmiṇī’s father) by offering your personal valour as the dowry (i.e. by defeating the opposition) as also the absolvement of Nṛga[3] from sin at Dvārakā.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Nṛga—A king of Ikṣvāku race, famous for his gifts of cows to Brāhmaṇas. One day, due to oversight and without being aware of it, he gifted a cow which was already given by him to another Brāhmaṇa. For this sin, he was born as a Chameleon but was absolved of all sins at the touch of Kṛṣṇa and went to heaven—vide infra 10.64.10.

devanagari: उद्वाहं वीरकन्यानां वीर्यशुल्कादिलक्षणम् । नृगस्य मोक्षणं शापाद् द्वारकायां जगत्पते ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: udvāhaṃ vīrakanyānāṃ vīryaśulkādilakṣaṇam nṛgasya mokṣaṇaṃ śāpād dvārakāyāṃ jagatpate
translation: Thereafter I would see the recovery of the jewel Syamantaka and a spouse (Jāmbavatī) along with it and the restoration of his son to a Brāhmaṇa from the abode of Death.

devanagari: स्यमन्तकस्य च मणेः आदानं सह भार्यया । मृतपुत्रप्रदानं च ब्राह्मणस्य स्वधामतः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: syamantakasya ca maṇeḥ ādānaṃ saha bhāryayā mṛtaputrapradānaṃ ca brāhmaṇasya svadhāmataḥ
translation: (I shall see later) the destruction of Pauṇḍraka,[4] and burning down the city of Kāśī, the death of Dantavaktra and slaying of Śiśupāla, the king of Cedi in the great sacrifice Rājasūya, performed by Yudhiṣṭhira).
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Pauṇḍraka: King of ancient Kārūṣa. His name was Vāsudeva. On hearing of Vāsudeva Kṛṣṇa, he sent him a message to surrender all his special royal emblem and worship him. Kṛṣṇa invaded Kārūṣa with a big army and killed Pauṇḍraka Vāsudeva who used to wield artificial Sudarśana discus etc. like Kṛṣṇa Vāsudeva.—vide infra 10.66.7-21

devanagari: पौण्ड्रकस्य वधं पश्चात् काशिपुर्याश्च दीपनम् । दन्तवक्रस्य निधनं चैद्यस्य च महाक्रतौ ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: pauṇḍrakasya vadhaṃ paścāt kāśipuryāśca dīpanam dantavakrasya nidhanaṃ caidyasya ca mahākratau
translation: It will be my privilege to see whatever other heroic deeds your Lordship would perform while dwelling in Dvārakā and they shall be eulogised in songs by poets on the earth.

devanagari: यानि चान्यानि वीर्याणि द्वारकामावसन् भवान् । कर्ता द्रक्ष्याम्यहं तानि गेयानि कविभिर्भुवि ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: yāni cānyāni vīryāṇi dvārakāmāvasan bhavān kartā drakṣyāmyahaṃ tāni geyāni kavibhirbhuvi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb]

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: After that I shall see you in the form of Death or time-spirit intent on destroying the whole universe and the annihilation of Akṣauhiṇīs (Divisions) of armies with you in the role of Arjuna’s charioteer.

devanagari: अथ ते कालरूपस्य क्षपयिष्णोरमुष्य वै । अक्षौहिणीनां निधनं द्रक्ष्याम्यर्जुनसारथेः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: atha te kālarūpasya kṣapayiṣṇoramuṣya vai akṣauhiṇīnāṃ nidhanaṃ drakṣyāmyarjunasāratheḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: We approach, for protection, the glorious Lord who is entirely constituted of perfectly pure knowledge and has all his purposes accomplished as he is well-established in his own blissful nature; whose will is never futile; and due to whose spiritual refulgence the stream of guṇas originating from Māyā is ever warded off.

devanagari: विशुद्धविज्ञानघनं स्वसंस्थया । समाप्तसर्वार्थममोघवाञ्छितम् । स्वतेजसा नित्यनिवृत्तमाया । गुणप्रवाहं भगवन्तमीमहि ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: viśuddhavijñānaghanaṃ svasaṃsthayā samāptasarvārthamamoghavāñchitam svatejasā nityanivṛttamāyā guṇapravāhaṃ bhagavantamīmahi
translation: I lay prostrate before you, the leader of Yadus, Vṛṣṇis and Sātvatas—the leader (yourself) who are the supreme Ruler, and who have brought forth all this varied creation (the phenomenal world of differences) through his own Māyā and have assumed a human form for the sake of sporting.

devanagari: त्वामीश्वरं स्वाश्रयमात्ममायया । विनिर्मिताशेषविशेषकल्पनम् । क्रीडार्थमद्यात्तमनुष्यविग्रहं । नतोऽस्मि धुर्यं यदुवृष्णिसात्वताम् ॥ २४ ...
english_devnagari: tvāmīśvaraṃ svāśrayamātmamāyayā vinirmitāśeṣaviśeṣakalpanam krīḍārthamadyāttamanuṣyavigrahaṃ nato
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
In this way, the sage Nārada, the foremost among the votaries who was delighted to see him, paid obeisance to Kṛṣṇa, the Chief Yadus and departed with his permission.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं यदुपतिं कृष्णं भागवतप्रवरो मुनिः । प्रणिपत्याभ्यनुज्ञातो ययौ तद्दर्शनोत्सवः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ yadupatiṃ kṛṣṇaṃ bhāgavatapravaro muniḥ praṇipatyābhyanujñāto yayau taddarśanotsavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: And Lord Kṛṣṇa also, having killed Keśin in a fight, tended the cows along with other cowherds who were highly pleased with him for his heroic act and enhanced the joy and happiness of the inhabitants of Vraja.

devanagari: भगवानपि गोविन्दो हत्वा केशिनमाहवे । पशूनपालयत् पालैः प्रीतैर्व्रजसुखावहः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavānapi govindo hatvā keśinamāhave paśūnapālayat pālaiḥ prītairvrajasukhāvahaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental plural from √ prī class 4 verb], [instrumental plural from √ prī class 9 verb]
[instrumental plural from √ prī class 4 verb], [instrumental plural from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation: On one occasion, while they were grazing the cattle on the table-land high up on the mountain, the cowherd boys played the game of hide and seek in which some gopas became thieves (or cattle-lifters) and others guards (or policemen).

devanagari: एकदा ते पशून् पालाःन् चारयन्तोऽद्रिसानुषु । चक्रुर्निलायनक्रीडाः चोरपालापदेशतः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: ekadā te paśūn pālāḥn cārayanto cakrurnilāyanakrīḍāḥ corapālāpadeśataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ car ], [vocative plural from √ car ]

translation: At that time, some played the part of thieves while others became guards, while some others played the role of sheep (to be lifted). In this way they indulged in the game fearlessly, Oh King!

devanagari: तत्रासन्कतिचिच्चोराः पालाश्च कतिचिन्नृप । मेषायिताश्च तत्रैके विजह्रुरकुतोभयाः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāsankaticiccorāḥ pālāśca katicinnṛpa meṣāyitāśca tatraike vijahrurakutobhayāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ i ], [vocative plural from √ i ]
[nominative plural from √ i ], [vocative plural from √ i ], [accusative plural from √ i ]

translation: Vyoma, the son of the Asura Maya who was a past-master in black magic, assumed the form of a cowherd and playing the role of a cattle-thief carried away a great many cowherd-boys who acted the part of goats.

devanagari: मयपुत्रो महामायो व्योमो गोपालवेषधृक् । मेषायितानपोवाह प्रायश्चोरायितो बहून् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: mayaputro mahāmāyo vyomo gopālaveṣadhṛk meṣāyitānapovāha prāyaścorāyito bahūn

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The mighty Asura threw each cowherd-boy so carried in the cave of that mountain, and closed its entrance with a rock. In this way four or five boys remained (outside the cave).

devanagari: गिरिदर्यां विनिक्षिप्य नीतं नीतं महासुरः । शिलया पिदधे द्वारं चतुःपञ्चावशेषिताः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: giridaryāṃ vinikṣipya nītaṃ nītaṃ mahāsuraḥ śilayā pidadhe dvāraṃ catuḥpañcāvaśeṣitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ nī class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ nī class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ nī class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ nī class 1 verb]

translation: Noticing the vile act of that Asura, Kṛṣṇa, the protector of the righteous, forcibly caught hold of him while kidnapping the cowherds, even as a lion would do to a wolf.

devanagari: तस्य तत्कर्म विज्ञाय कृष्णः शरणदः सताम् । गोपान् नयन्तं जग्राह वृकं हरिरिवौजसा ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya tatkarma vijñāya kṛṣṇaḥ śaraṇadaḥ satām gopān nayantaṃ jagrāha vṛkaṃ haririvaujasā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The mighty demon resumed his original form big like a mountain. Being suffocated with his grip, he wanted to extricate himself but could not do so.

devanagari: स निजं रूपमास्थाय गिरीन्द्रसदृशं बली । इच्छन् विमोक्तुमात्मानं नाशक्नोद् ग्रहणातुरः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa nijaṃ rūpamāsthāya girīndrasadṛśaṃ balī icchan vimoktumātmānaṃ nāśaknod grahaṇāturaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb]

translation: Catching hold of him by both his hands, he dashed the Asura to the ground and killed him in the way of sacrificial beasts, while gods were witnessing it from heaven.

devanagari: तं निगृह्याच्युतो दोर्भ्यां पातयित्वा महीतले । पश्यतां दिवि देवानां पशुमारममारयत् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ nigṛhyācyuto dorbhyāṃ pātayitvā mahītale paśyatāṃ divi devānāṃ paśumāramamārayat
translation:
Having broken open the rock that blocked the entrance to the cave, he brought the cowherds out of that perilous situation. Glorified by gods and cowherds, he returned to his own Gokula.
[back to top]

devanagari: गुहापिधानं निर्भिद्य गोपान् निःसार्य कृच्छ्रतः । स्तूयमानः सुरैर्गोपैः प्रविवेश स्वगोकुलम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: guhāpidhānaṃ nirbhidya gopān niḥsārya kṛcchrataḥ stūyamānaḥ surairgopaiḥ praviveśa svagokulam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे पूर्वार्धे व्योमासुरवधो नाम सप्तत्रिंशोऽध्यायः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe pūrvārdhe vyomāsuravadho nāma saptatriṃśo
